Class IntrospectionImplSkeleton<I_SELF extends IntrospectionImplSkeleton<I_SELF,I,B,S>, I extends IntrospectionSkeleton<I,S>, B extends IntrospectionImplSkeleton<I_SELF,I,B,S>.Builder, S extends Scope>

java.lang.Object
dev.goldmensch.propane.IntrospectionImplSkeleton<I_SELF,I,B,S>
All Implemented Interfaces:
IntrospectionSkeleton<I,S>
public abstract class IntrospectionImplSkeleton<I_SELF extends IntrospectionImplSkeleton<I_SELF,I,B,S>, I extends IntrospectionSkeleton<I,S>, B extends IntrospectionImplSkeleton<I_SELF,I,B,S>.Builder, S extends Scope> extends Object implements IntrospectionSkeleton<I,S>

Propane differentiates its API into two 2 sides:

  • the one accessible by the user of the library that is using propane
  • the one only accessible by the library using propane, so to speak the "internal" API

IntrospectionImpl is this second, internal side of Introspection. Here a library can register PropertyProvider and create child instances of other IntrospectionImpl. Therefore, you should read the documentation of Introspection first.

The first step of creating an IntrospectionImpl is by calling #create(Scope) with the "root" scope passed on a specific version of this class. In most cases, this is the Scope with the lowest priority. Looking at the example in Scope, this would be Scopes.CONFIGURATION.

For any child scope, you create a child Introspection instance by calling createChild(Scope) on the parent one with the new scope passed. This child instance will have all property valus of the parent scope and its own property values. For furhrter information, take a look at the documentation of Introspection and Scope.

Beside properties, this class is also used to publish Events. Such events, must have the same Scope as the introspection instance. The published events are then passed to the parent of this class and so on.

See Also:
  • why you have to use the "specific" version of this class

  • Method Details

    • addIntrospectionProvider

      protected abstract void addIntrospectionProvider(Properties<I> properties)

    • create

      public static IntrospectionImplSkeleton<?,?,?,?>.Builder create(Scope scope)
      Creates an instance of this IntrospectionImpl with the given Scope set.
      Parameters:
      scope - the Scope of the to be created IntrospectionImpl
      Returns:
      the IntrospectionImplSkeleton.Builder of the new IntrospectionImpl

    • get

      public <T> T get(SpecificProperty<T> specific)
      Returns the value for the requested property by either retrieving it from the cache or computing it according to the class' documentation.
      Specified by:
      get in interface IntrospectionSkeleton<I_SELF extends IntrospectionImplSkeleton<I_SELF,I,B,S>, I extends IntrospectionSkeleton<I,S>>
      Parameters:
      specific - the requested property
      Returns:
      the value of the requested property
      See Also:
      • Introspections' class documentation

    • scope

      public S scope()
      Returns the Scope this introspection instance is bound to.
      Specified by:
      scope in interface IntrospectionSkeleton<I_SELF extends IntrospectionImplSkeleton<I_SELF,I,B,S>, I extends IntrospectionSkeleton<I,S>>
      Returns:
      the Scope of this introspection instance

    • createChild

      public abstract B createChild(S scope)
      Creates a child introspection instance with the given scope, that must be a child of scope that this introspection instance is bound to.
      Parameters:
      scope - the Scope of the child instance
      Returns:
      a builder to create the introspection instance

    • subscribe

      public void subscribe(Listener<? extends Event<S>, S, I> listener)
      Subscribes to an event with the given Listener. The provided listener will be stored in this instance, thus be available for the lifetime of this introspection instance.
      Specified by:
      subscribe in interface IntrospectionSkeleton<I_SELF extends IntrospectionImplSkeleton<I_SELF,I,B,S>, I extends IntrospectionSkeleton<I,S>>
      Parameters:
      listener - the Listener to be registered
      See Also:
      • Introspections' class documentation

    • publish

      public void publish(Event<S> event)

      Publishes the given event to this introspection and its parents.

      All listeners registered on this introspection instance or any parent instance, that are listening for the type of this event will be called.

      Parameters:
      event - the Event to be published
